answer:
\boxed{G}  

The Breusch-Godfrey test is specifically designed to test for autocorrelation in the residuals of a regression model, and it can be applied to test for autocorrelation of any order, including up to the third order. In contrast, the Durbin-Watson test only checks for first-order autocorrelation, while the other listed tests serve different purposes such as stationarity, normality, heteroskedasticity, or model specification.
